examples: Hmmm.Stephen, is that a good Hebrew or Aramaic name?
Aramaic is a Semitic language and it was the day-to-day language of Israel from 539 BC - 70 AD.
Not pictured are the developments of Aramaic and Nabatean, which led to the modern Arabic script.
Theoretically, any document written originally in Greek, would not translate easily into Aramaic or Hebrew.
And it seems to be an Aramaic that fits with that of the Dead Sea Scrolls.
